## [39:50 - 42:54]

method is, and I talked about this in that video training, is that when you do that, you're at serious risk of them saying, oh, I got so much good stuff and here I've got your formula. One of them is the tips content where you just go give three tips to do better yoga. Or the formula is my second one. find an answer, but generally you're going to go over the same problems. Like you're just going to beat yourself up. or the communication of the value that they get. So I'm just cautioning you that every time I've done a survey, 50% of the answers are it's too expensive. I couldn't afford it, something like that. So here's the way I look at that. First off, I just want to make a comment. I know Walt, I know you. I know you didn't word it this way because you don't understand it. It's all transactional versus what I'm saying is you need to get relationship building and trust building into that process. To me, it's not a balance between the two. Okay, here we go. This is a good one too. Teresa asks, is there a time a month or year that we should avoid? I'm a nutritionist and I work with people with major food and weight problems. I'm scheduled September through October, but I would like to offer a course about handling food through the holidays beginning November to mid-January. Will I find any problems with that? So, Donna, I'm going to turn this over to you after I say this one thing. So, her very first question, is there a time of the month or year that we should avoid? So, a couple of things. One of the things that we've learned is that products tend to sell better when they're offered just after payday. Yeah. And most people get paid on the first of the month. Whether they get paid in the middle of the month or not, most people get paid on the end. And so, webinars done on like the 26, 27, 28, 29th. If your cart closes before the end of the month, you usually will hurt your cells a little bit. If you can slide it a week later so your cart will close after their next payday,

## [1:10:24 - 1:13:24]

you'll tend to do better unless it's something like the July 4th holiday happening in there. Memorial Day, July 4th are the two problem holidays because of where they happen. And so, holidays. Remember, we sold this just before July 4th too. Okay. Now, time of year that you should avoid. Generally, I don't like doing launches between mid-November and January 1st. There's lots of things on people's minds. They are spending a lot of money during that time. So, they feel they're particularly in scarcity mode during that time. There is one exception. And that is if you are in the weight loss or health niche. You definitely want to be doing some marketing during that time. And, Teresa, that's you. So, in some ways. And it's perfect for you. The framing you want to have is, I mean, again, transformation, transformation, transformation is that they don't fall into the common traps.
